line_preview: # Please do not remove any comments from this file, but feel free to add as many
# comments to R code as you like!
# Replace all ---USER INPUT--- sections with relevant input (including the -
# When you are done, save this file as RPCB_Final_Analysis_Study_XX.R, where XX
# "Study 1: Replication of Poliseno et al., 2010 (Nature)", should be
#@ Original Study Title: Fusobacterium nucleatum infection is prevalent in human colorectal carcinoma
#@ eLife link to Registered Report: https://doi.org/10.7554/eLife.10012
#@ eLife link to Replication Study: https://doi.org/10.7554/eLife.25801
#@ Experiment and Effect Numbers: Experiment 1 (1 effect)
#@ NOTE: Data must be loaded from OSF directly and NOT rely on any local files.
# included in analysis script 'Study_50_combined_qPCR_statistical_analysis.R' from https://osf.io/8vcea/
# original p value and sample size in meta-analysis script 'Study_50_meta_analysis.R' from https://osf.io/2td6k/
#@ If NO (Original data is not representative):
#@ NOTE: Perform these for Replication data and put NA for original data if original data is representative
#@ NOTE: Perform these for Original data if replication was attempted, but data uninterpretable
#@ NOTE: Include a print or summary call on the resulting object
#@ Original study statistical test: Paired t-test (ratio t-test)
# Ratio t-test is a paired t-test on the logarithm of the ratio of the treated/control,
# which is a paired t test on the logarithms of the values.
# Downloads R script file 'Study_50_meta_analysis.R' from https://osf.io/2td6k/
#@ Replication study statistical test: Wilcoxon signed-rank test
# Downloads R script file 'Study_50_combined_qPCR_statistical_analysis.R' from https://osf.io/8vcea/
